UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A GENERAL DEMURRER DOES NOT LIE TO ONLY PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. IF THERE ARE SUFFICIENT ALLEGATIONS TO ENTITLE PLAINTIFF TO RELIEF, OTHER ALLEGATIONS CANNOT BE CHALLENGED BY GENERAL DEMURRER. SEE KONG V. CITY OF HAWAIIAN GARDENS REDEVELOP. AGENCY (2003) 108 CAL. APP. 4TH 1028, 1046. DEFENDANT DID NOT BRING A SPECIAL DEMURRER PER CCP 430.10(F) RE: CRC 2.112 COMPLIANCE. DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL ARE ORDERED AGAIN (SEE CGC-12-527342 MARCH 12, 2013 TENTATIVE RULING) TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6C IN FUTURE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PAUL H. ALVARADO, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S MOTION TO DISSOLVE THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION ISSUED ON APRIL 21, 2014 IS HEREBY DISSOLVED. ACCORDINGLY, WELLS FARGO AND IT'S AGENTS MAY PROCEED WITH THE NON-JUDICIAL FORECLOSURE ON THE SUBJECT PROPERTY, LOCATED AT 1518 34TH AVENUE, SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94122. WELLS FARGO MAY PROCEED WOTH A TRUSTEE'S SALE WITH REGARDS TO THE SUBJECT PROPERTY, ON OR AFTER AUGUST 4, 2014. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ALICE CHANG, CSR#13654, (415-591-3333), REPORTED. (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2015 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2014 Continued To Oct-19-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings And Appendix
Off Calendar Mar-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Rapkine, Esq. of AFRCT, (626-535-1900), for Wells Fargo Bank, Defendant and moving party.
Christopher J. Gonzalez, Esq. of Timken Johnson LLP, (510-833-2125), for Julio Borruso, Plaintiff and opposing party.
Reporter Angela Sinclair, CSR #13902, (209-602-8366).

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Appendix is
GRANTED. Plaintiff's action is pre-empted by HOLA. Court grants the request of plaintiff for leave to amend the complaint within ten days from the notice of entry of order. Prevailing party to prepare order and judgment in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Reporter Angela Sinclair, CSR #13902, (209-602-8366), D501.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings And Appendix
Off Calendar Feb-17-2015 Continued To Mar-05-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings And Appendix
Off Calendar Feb-13-2015 Continued To Feb-25-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S MOTION For Judgment On The Pleadings And Appendix is continued to February 25, 2015 on Court's own motion. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings And Appendix
Off Calendar Dec-31-2014 Continued To Feb-13-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-26-2014 Set For Trial On Mar-23-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dissolve The Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Gonzales, Esq. (925-945-6211) of Timken Johnson LLP for Julio Borruso, Plaintiff and opposing party.
Michael Rapkine, Esq. (626-535-1900) of AFRCT for Wells Fargo Bank, Defendant and moving party.
Court Reporter: Alice Chang, CSR#13654, (415-591-3333), Reported. (D501)

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S Motion To Dissolve The Preliminary Injunction is Granted. The preliminary injunction issued on April 21, 2014 is hereby dissolved. Accordingly, Wells Fargo and it's agents may proceed with the non-judicial foreclosure on the subject property, located at 1518 34th Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94122. Wells Fargo may proceed woth a trustee's sale with regards to the subject property, on or after August 4, 2014. Order is signed in open court.Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Alice Chang, CSR#13654, (415-591-3333), Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Ccp 2924.12
Off Calendar Jun-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Gonzalez, Esq. of Timken Johnson LLP, (925-945-6211), for Julio Borruso & Angelita Borruso, Plaintiffs and moving parties.
Michael Rapkine, Esq. of AFRCT, (626-535-1900), for Wells Fargo Bank, Defendant and opposing party.
Reporter Rick Galten, #13202, (415-591-3333).

Court issues a ruling prior to the hearing granting Plaintiffs' motion for attorney's fees and costs and requiring hearing to address the amount. Plaintiff argues on the amount. Defendant argues on the ruling and the amount. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: PLAINTIFFS JULIO BORRUSO and ANGELITA BORRUSO's Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Ccp 2924.12 is taken under submission. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. A general demurrer does not lie to only part of a cause of action. If there are sufficient allegations to entitle plaintiff to relief, other allegations cannot be challenged by general demurrer. See Kong v. City of Hawaiian Gardens Redevelop. Agency (2003) 108 Cal. App. 4th 1028, 1046. Defendant did not bring a special demurrer per CCP 430.10(f) re: CRC 2.112 compliance. Defendant and its counsel are ordered again (See CGC-12-527342 March 12, 2013 tentative ruling) to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Paul H. Alvarado, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Apr-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances on Friday, April 11, 2014:
Christopher J. Gonzalez, Esq. of Timken Johnson, LLP (925.945.6211) for Plaintiffs JULIO BORRUSO and ANGELITA BORRUSO;
Michael Rapkine, Esq. of Angelin Flewelling Rasmussen Campbell & Trytten, LLP (626.535.1900) for Defendant WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.

Hearing re: Order to Show Cause re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order. The Court issues the following tentative ruling: The preliminary injunction is GRANTED. Defendants are enjoined from proceeding with non-judicial foreclosure until Plaintiffs' loan modification application is evaluated as of July 2013 based on inputs provided in July 9, 2013 letter and third-party appraisal to provide plaintiffs' meaningful opportunity to obtain loan modification. Nothing in this order shall be interpreted to require particular result of that process. Plaintiffs shall post $24,164.50 with this Court no later than April 25 2014. Planitiffs shall post $2,416.45 on the first of each months for the duration of the preliminary injunction. Defendant may bring a motion to dissolve this preliminary injunction at any time after compliance with this order. The Court, having reviewed briefs submitted and hearing oral argument by respective counsel, adopts the tentative ruling. Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk L. Fong; Reporter: Alice Chang, CSR 13654, Esquire Deposition Solutions, Tel.: 415.591.1333. (501)

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Continued To Apr-11-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
